title: calycera crassifolia
intro: Calycera crassifolia, also known as the rough-seed marsh-barrel, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Campanulaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calycera crassifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Calycera crassifolia often include infusions and decoctions, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Calycera crassifolia may have immunostimulatory properties, which can increase the production of anti-inflammatory interleukin-10 (IL-10) and enhance the immune system.
